demo1.title: Mission Control
beats: You're not running one AI agent anymore. You're running a fleet.
Codecast is the command center for all of them. Every session, every project, in one place.
This is your inbox. Pinned work sits up top, then the list that actually matters. The sessions waiting on you.
Codecast reads each agent's state, and surfaces only the ones where the ball is in your court.
Every card tells the story at a glance. What it's working on, which model it's running, how deep the conversation goes.
Switch between projects without ever losing the thread.
One screen. Your entire agent fleet. Nothing slips through.

demo2.title: Talk to Your Fleet
beats: Every agent session in Codecast is a teammate you can talk to.
From your terminal, message any session by its short ID. Old or active, running or asleep.
It lands as a new turn. The agent wakes up with full context, and gets to work.
Be the boss. Hand a dormant session a task, and it resumes and runs it.
Or be a peer. Trade updates on a shared problem, and ask one to ping you when it's done.
The message feed gathers every message across every session into one timeline.
Your whole fleet. One conversation.

demo3.title: Branch and Parallelize
beats: The best idea isn't always the obvious one. So don't pick just one. Run them all.
Fork a conversation, and it branches. Each copy keeps the full history, then chases its own direction.
Or spawn fresh sessions. Parallel work, no shared history, each on its own task.
They start immediately, and land right in your inbox, side by side.
This is a real fleet. Sixty-four sessions running at once, every one live.
Explore three directions in the time it used to take to try one.

demo4.title: Structured Work
beats: Agents do the work. But who is keeping track of all of it?
Codecast turns loose sessions into structured work. Tasks and plans a human can actually follow.
Every task has an owner, a priority, and a status you can see at a glance.
Bundle a big effort into a plan. Agents claim tasks, log their progress, and mark them done.
Grouped by who's carrying it, so you see your whole team's load in one view.
From a thousand scattered messages, to one clear board.

demo5.title: Agents That Never Sleep
beats: The most powerful agents are the ones still running while you sleep.
Triggers are scheduled agent runs. Recurring, one-time, or fired by an event like a new pull request comment.
Here's the next twenty-four hours of your fleet, laid out on a single timeline.
Each one wakes on schedule, does its job, and reports back. A daily health check. A churn monitor. An experiment that reads its own results.
And when one job needs an army, workflows fan out.
This research run spun up over a hundred agents. Searching, fetching, verifying, all in parallel.
Codecast. Command your agents. Even when you're not there.
